1.description: Price-dividend ratio, return volatility, instantaneous expected return, and agentA$A$'s portfolio. In the case of time-varying disagreement case, we plot all quantities as functions of dividend(Dt)$(D_t)$att=0.1$t=0.1$. In each panel, the gray vertical lines denote the circuit breaker thresholdD̲t${\,\underline{\!D\!\!}\,\,}_t$, and the gray dashed lines correspond to the two cases with only one of the agents present in the market. The variables of interest are conditional onDt$D_t$. The parameters areT=1$T=1$,μ=0.04%$\mu =0.04\%$,σ=ν=3%$\sigma =\nu = 3\%$,κ=0$\kappa =0$,δ0=δ¯=0$\delta _0 = \bar {\delta }=0$,ω=0.9$\omega =0.9$, andα=5%$\alpha =5\%$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: The “magnet effect.”In the case of time-varying disagreement, we plot the conditional probabilities for the stock price to reach the circuit breaker threshold within the nexth$h$minutes fromt=0.1$t=0.1$. Here,h$h$is proportional to(St−S̲)2$(S_{t} -\underline {S})^2$:h$h$is roughly 0.5 minutes whenSt=0.95$S_t =0.95$, 9.5 minutes whenSt=0.97$S_t =0.97$, and 62 minutes whenSt=1.01$S_t = 1.01$. The black circle representsS0$S_0$. The gray vertical bar denotes the circuit breaker thresholdS̲$\underline {S}$. The parameters areμ=0.04%$\mu =0.04\%$,σ=ν=3%$\sigma =\nu = 3\%$,κ=0$\kappa =0$,δ0=δ¯=0$\delta _0 = \bar {\delta }=0$,ω=0.9$\omega =0.9$, andα=5%$\alpha =5\%$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Model predicted one-minute conditional volatility, conditional skewness, and conditional expected return. In the case of time-varying disagreement, we plot all model predicted quantities as functions of the distance to the circuit breaker threshold (DTCB, defined asSt/S0−(1−α)$S_t/S_0 - (1-\alpha)$). Both volatility and return are normalized to one day. The parameters areμ=0.04%$\mu =0.04\%$,σ=ν=3%$\sigma =\nu = 3\%$,κ=0$\kappa =0$,δ0=δ¯=0$\delta _0 = \bar {\delta }=0$,ω=0.9$\omega =0.9$, andα=5%$\alpha =5\%$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: Welfare impact. Agents' gain in certainty equivalent wealth in a market with the circuit breaker versus a market without for different initial wealth sharesω$\omega$of agentA$A$(the case of time-varying disagreement). The blue dashed line corresponds to agentA$A$and the green dotted line corresponds to agentB$B$. The red solid line corresponds to the entire market, with proper weights for the agents. In Panel A, the certainty equivalent wealth is defined under agents' own beliefs. In Panel B, it is defined under the objective probability measure. The parameters areμ=0.04%$\mu =0.04\%$,σ=3%$\sigma =3\%$,ν=6%$\nu =6\%$,κ=0$\kappa =0$,δ0=δ¯=0$\delta _0 = \bar {\delta }=0$,ω=0.9$\omega =0.9$, andα=5%$\alpha =5\%$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Welfare trade-offs. In the case of time-varying disagreement, the left panel shows the change in total welfare due to the circuit breaker in the presence of two types of trading motives: risk-sharing and irrational speculation. The right panel shows the dependence of the change in total welfare on the circuit breaker level. The parameters areμ=0.04%$\mu =0.04\%$,σ=3%$\sigma =3\%$,ν=6%$\nu =6\%$,κ=0$\kappa =0$, andδ0=δ¯=0$\delta _0 = \bar {\delta }=0$. In the left panel,α=5%$\alpha =5\%$; in the right panel,ω=0.50$\omega =0.50$andωB=0.41$\omega ^B =0.41$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: Binned scatterplot on regression coefficients of (40). This figure shows theβ$\beta$regression coefficients of (40) for volatility (two-scale realized volatility), skewness, expected return, and abnormal volume.Bin1=(0%,0.75%]$\text{Bin}_1=(0\%, 0.75\%]$,Bin2=(0.75%,1.25%]$\text{Bin}_2 = (0.75\%, 1.25\%]$,Bin3=(1.25%,2%]$\text{Bin}_3 = (1.25\%, 2\%]$, andBini=((i−1)%,i%]$\text{Bin}_i = ((i-1)\%, i\%]$fori=4,⋯,14$i=4, \dots, 14$. The red dots indicateβ$\beta$coefficients and the gray regions represent two standard deviations around theβ$\beta$coefficients. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

7.description: Stock price at timeτ$\tau$with positive bond supply. Blue solid lines correspond to the case with the circuit breaker. Red dotted lines correspond to the case without the circuit breaker. The parameters areτ=0.25$\tau =0.25$,Dτ=0.97$D_\tau = 0.97$, andΔ=0.17$\Delta = 0.17$. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

8.description: Impact of net bond supply on volatility. Heat map for the ratio of average daily volatilities in the economies with and without circuit breakers. For normalization, we subtract one from the ratios.
